steps.title: Spot the contrast word
steps.content: The word "novel" signals the researchers chose a new and different path. This only stands out as novel if existing studies represent what's standard.

steps.title: Match the standard
steps.content:
Substitute each word into "many ______ studies" and test against the passage's contrast with "novel":

A. Eccentric means bizarrely unconventional. "Many eccentric studies" would already be unusual, so the researchers' "novel path" wouldn't stand out as different — the contrast collapses.

B. Meager means small in quantity. The passage says nothing about how many prior studies exist; the blank describes the type of prior approach, not its amount.

C. Conventional means following the standard, accepted approach. "Many conventional studies focus on people above sea level" sets up a clear contrast with the "novel path" of studying people below sea level. ✓

D. Random means without plan or pattern. The prior studies were systematic enough to have a consistent focus (altitude above sea level); calling them random contradicts the passage.

C is correct.
